items.title: Three Things I Noticed in Knicks-Spurs Game 2

items.body_excerpt: Hit the jump for the Game 2 edition of Three Things, starring Mikal Bridges, Karl-Anthony Towns, and De'Aaron Fox.

items.title: I wrote about what the wild end to Game 2 means for Wemby—and everything that led up to that moment, from the Spurs' scoring struggles to the return of Brooklyn Bridges to the great shrinking of San Antonio's rotation
items.author: Zach Kram
items.body_excerpt:
I wrote about what the wild end to Game 2 means for Wemby—and everything that led up to that moment, from the Spurs' scoring struggles to the return of Brooklyn Bridges to the great shrinking of San Antonio's rotation

www.espn.com/nba/story/_/...

images.alt:
Both attempts came between 20 and 21 feet away from the basket, with a close defender. Entering Friday's game, Wembanyama had made 34% (11-for-32) of shots fitting those criteria in his career, per GeniusIQ. Similarly, the tracking data says the shooter-specific quantified shot probability on his end-of-game attempts in Game 2 was 36%.

That's essentially a pair of one-in-three chances to win a Finals game. Wembanyama missed them both.

But that's how the ball bounces in a make-or-miss sport; it's a narrow margin on the highest stage, and titles have been decided by less. As Wembanyama told reporters after the game, "In moments like this, results matter more than process. ... I need to score."

items.title: If you want to know why the Knicks have a 2-0 lead, watch what's going on away from the ball.
items.author: Steph Noh
items.body_excerpt:
If you want to know why the Knicks have a 2-0 lead, watch what's going on away from the ball. 

Wemby wasn't shut down in the first half because the moment was too big. Rather, it was a disciplined gameplan by the Knicks to aggressively tag and disrupt his rolls. 

Turn 🔊 on:

items.title: June 6, 2001: Allen Iverson finishes with 48 PTS/6 AST/5 STL in Philadelphia's 107-101 (OT) win over the Lakers in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 6, 2001: Allen Iverson finishes with 48 PTS/6 AST/5 STL in Philadelphia's 107-101 (OT) win over the Lakers in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Also for the Sixers, Dikembe Mutombo had 13 PTS/16 REB/5 BLK. Shaquille O'Neal led Los Angeles with 44 PTS/20 REB/5 AST.

items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns in the 2026 playoffs:
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Karl-Anthony Towns in the 2026 playoffs:
17 PPG - 11 RPG - 6 APG - 1.1 SPG - 1.3 BPG on 70% TS 

Only one other player has done that: 
David Robinson. 

That’s the list.

(via E. Baraheni)
#NBA

items.title: A thing that's blowing my mind -- one of the million -- is that the Knicks have now won eight straight road playoff games, and have won a road game in seven straight series, and nine of the 11 in the Brunson era. They've gotten to that "you just know Steph will get one on the roa
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: A thing that's blowing my mind -- one of the million -- is that the Knicks have now won eight straight road playoff games, and have won a road game in seven straight series, and nine of the 11 in the Brunson era. They've gotten to that "you just know Steph will get one on the road" place.

items.title: The Knicks have eight or nine starters, while the Spurs only have five. This seems unfair. But it’s playing out in the Finals. No matter who Mike Brown calls on, he’s getting elite minutes from them, including Friday’s nailbiter. Free to read, in @theathletic.com:

items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns last night:
items.author: Justin Kubatko
items.body_excerpt:
Karl-Anthony Towns last night:

✅ 21 PTS
✅ 13 REB
✅ 8-12 FG
✅ 3-5 3P

Towns joined Dirk Nowitzki as the only listed 7-footers in NBA history to make 100 career 3P in the playoffs.

He reached that mark in 66 games, 15 fewer than Nowitzki.

Read, share, subscribe ⬇️
